title: tagetes subvillosa
intro: Tagetes subvillosa, also known as the dwarf Mexican marigold, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Mexico and Central America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat wounds, burns, and other skin conditions. The health benefits of Tagetes subvillosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall well-being. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
